description: A taxonomy of narrative manipulation tactics for analysis.

tactics.name: Ad Hominem
tactics.description: Attacking the opponent's character or personal traits in an attempt to undermine their argument, rather than addressing the substance of the argument itself.

tactics.name: Straw Man
tactics.description: Misrepresenting someone's argument to make it easier to attack. This involves exaggerating, misrepresenting, or completely fabricating someone's argument.

tactics.name: Appeal to Emotion
tactics.description: Manipulating an emotional response in place of a valid or compelling argument. This includes appeals to fear, anger, pity, or patriotism.
indicators: our way of life is under attack

tactics.name: Whataboutism
tactics.description: Attempting to discredit an opponent's position by charging them with hypocrisy without directly refuting or disproving their argument. It's a variant of the tu quoque fallacy.

tactics.name: Gaslighting
tactics.description: Psychologically manipulating someone into questioning their own sanity, perception of reality, or memories. In discourse, it involves denying or distorting reality to sow confusion.

tactics.name: Cherry-Picking (Suppressed Evidence)
tactics.description: The act of pointing to individual cases or data that seem to confirm a particular position, while ignoring a significant portion of related cases or data that may contradict that position.

tactics.name: False Dichotomy (False Dilemma)
tactics.description: Presenting two alternative states as the only possibilities, when in fact more possibilities exist. It frames an issue as an 'either/or' choice, when it's more complex.

tactics.name: Slippery Slope
tactics.description: Arguing that a relatively small first step will inevitably lead to a chain of related events culminating in some significant (usually negative) effect.
indicators: it starts with .*, then leads to

tactics.name: Fear-mongering
tactics.description: The deliberate use of fear to influence public opinion or achieve a political goal. It often involves exaggerating dangers and arousing public alarm.
